name: Saint-Médard and Salaunes Cycling Loop
name_original: Salaunes et Landes de Bordeaux - boucle dans le Parc naturel régional du Médoc
description: This easy 24.9-mile (40.1 km) touring cycling loop in the Médoc Regional Natural Park offers gentle, car-free paths through forests and vine
region_intro: Touring cycling around Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc offers a network of routes through varied landscapes. The area is characterized by relatively flat terrain, extensive forests like the Bois du Déhès, and vineyards typical of the Médoc region. Located near Bordeaux, it benefits from proximity to the Médoc Regional Natural Park and the Atlantic coast, providing diverse cycling environments. Best touring cycling routes around Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc The most popular touring cycling route is Saint-Médard and Salaunes Cycling Loop, an easy 24.9 miles (40.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This route navigates through local forests and quiet roads. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Salaunes Picnic Area – Salaunes Town Center loop from Saint-Aubin-de-Médoc, an easy 16.9 miles (27.2 km) path. This route offers a gentle ride through the local countryside, connecting small towns. Local touring cyclists also love the Salaunes Picnic Area – Salaunes Town Center loop from Allée de la Chambre, a 26.5 miles (42.7 km) trail leading through forests and open fields, often completed in about 2 hours 26 minutes. Touring cycling around Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc is defined by flat roads, extensive forests, and vineyards. The network offers options for both easy rides and moderate challenges. The routes in Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 450 reviews. More than 3000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc?
A: The region is known for its relatively flat terrain, making it ideal for touring cycling. Routes often traverse extensive forests, such as the Bois du Déhès, and the characteristic vineyards of the Médoc region. You'll find a good mix of dedicated cycle paths and quiet roads.

Q: Are there many cycling routes available in Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc?
A: Yes, there are numerous options for touring cyclists. The komoot community has mapped 16 touring cycling routes in the area, with 11 rated as easy and 5 as moderate, ensuring choices for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc are suitable for families due to the generally flat terrain and dedicated cycle paths. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15213666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Engineer's Park – La Gare cycliste loop from Saint-Médard-en-Jalles&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 9.4 km ride, perfect for a shorter family outing. The Bordeaux-Lacanau cycle path also offers car-free sections.

Q: What are some notable natural features or attractions to see along the cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can explore a variety of natural and historical attractions. Highlights include the extensive forests and vineyards, as well as the renowned &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5184863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Majolan Park&lt;/a&gt;, known for its artificial caves and beautiful gardens. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3116088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marshes of Bruges&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3077245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grunères Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular cycling routes in Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966629730/saint-medard-and-salaunes-cycling-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Médard and Salaunes Cycling Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 40.1 km circular route that takes you through local forests and quiet roads.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc?
A: The touring cycling routes in Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 450 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the quiet roads, the peaceful forest sections, and the well-maintained paths that make for enjoyable rides.

Q: Are there any routes that connect small towns or offer a glimpse into local life?
A: Yes, several routes are designed to connect local towns and villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41664835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salaunes Picnic Area – Salaunes Town Center loop from Saint-Aubin-de-Médoc&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 27.2 km path that offers a gentle ride through the local countryside, linking small communities and providing a taste of the regional atmosphere.

Q: What is the Bordeaux-Lacanau cycle path and does it pass near Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4463641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordeaux-Lacanau cycle path&lt;/a&gt; is a significant greenway connecting Bordeaux to the Atlantic coast. It features car-free sections and amenities like shaded rest areas. While it doesn't pass directly through Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc, it is easily accessible from the area, particularly from Saint-Médard-en-Jalles, and offers a fantastic cycling experience.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region has historical elements. Along some routes, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6393903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Saint-Médard-en-Jalles station&lt;/a&gt;, which has been repurposed with amenities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1113799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Raphael Chapel&lt;/a&gt; is another historical point of interest that can be seen on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29112102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château Lascombe – Saint Raphael Chapel loop from Saint-Médard-en-Jalles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the best routes for a moderate cycling challenge?
A: For those seeking a moderate challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41666718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salaunes Picnic Area – Salaunes Town Center loop from Allée de la Chambre&lt;/a&gt; offer a longer ride of 42.7 km through forests and open fields. This route provides a good balance of distance and varied scenery without significant elevation changes.

Q: Is it possible to cycle through vineyards in the Médoc region from Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc?
A: Yes, the Médoc region is famous for its vineyards, and many cycling routes will take you through or alongside these iconic landscapes. While Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc itself was historically pastoral with vineyards, the broader Médoc territory offers numerous châteaux and wine estates that can be admired from your bike.

Q: Are there any specific routes that highlight the forests around Saint-Aubin-De-Médoc?
A: Many routes emphasize the region's extensive forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966629730/saint-medard-and-salaunes-cycling-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Médard and Salaunes Cycling Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, navigating through local woodlands and quiet roads, offering a peaceful and shaded cycling experience.
tips: Nice little church with tables nearby for a picnic break.
On the way to Lacanau by the cycleway, you can stop here for the lunch

This greenway is known to take us to the ocean front. You will find in Samos a shaded rest area with water and something to repair your bike if you have a problem.
Small subtlety: this greenway is a corridor facing the ocean. If you take it towards Lacanau and the tide rises, you will have the wind in your face, if it goes down you will have the wind at your back.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Salaunes et Landes de Bordeaux loop?
faqs.answer: This touring cycling loop is rated as easy. The Médoc Regional Natural Park, where the route is located, is largely flat with minimal gradients, making it ideal for cyclists of all abilities. Many sections utilize dedicated cycle paths, ensuring a relaxed and accessible experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Salaunes et Landes de Bordeaux loop?
faqs.answer: While a specific starting point with parking isn't detailed for this exact loop, the region generally offers good access. For routes in the Médoc Regional Natural Park, look for parking near villages or designated cycle path access points. Salaunes itself would be a logical starting and parking point for this loop.

faqs.question: Is the Salaunes et Landes de Bordeaux loop suitable for families and beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is very suitable for families and beginners. The terrain is mostly flat, and many cycling routes in the Médoc Regional Natural Park are car-free, providing a safe and enjoyable environment for all ages and skill levels. The overall easy difficulty rating confirms its accessibility.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Salaunes et Landes de Bordeaux loop incorporates sections of several official cycling paths. Notably, it uses parts of the Voie Verte Bordeaux - Lacanau Océan and various segments of the Piste Cyclable Bordeaux - Lacanau. You may also encounter parts of Super Balade 5 - Sur la route du Médoc, ReVE 12 - St Médard en Jalles - St Vincent de Paul, and ReVE 1 - Bordeaux - St Aubin du Médoc.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: You can expect mostly flat and gentle terrain. The Médoc Regional Natural Park is characterized by extensive cycle paths, often converted railway lines, which means they have minimal gradients. The surfaces are generally paved, making for a smooth touring cycling experience.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic features along the Salaunes et Landes de Bordeaux loop?
faqs.answer: The loop takes you through the varied and picturesque scenery of the Médoc. You'll cycle through vast pine forests, which provide cool, shaded sections, and may also pass by some of the region's famous vineyards. The 'Landes' landscape, characterized by heathlands and forests, is a significant feature of the area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Salaunes et Landes de Bordeaux loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this loop is generally during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summer can be warm, but the extensive pine forests offer welcome shade. Winters are typically mild but can be damp.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by a few interesting spots. You'll find the historic Saint Raphael Chapel and a convenient Salaunes Picnic Area. A significant part of the route also follows the Bordeaux - Lacanau cycle path, which is a highlight in itself.

faqs.question: Is the Salaunes et Landes de Bordeaux loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome on many outdoor paths in France, especially on leash, specific regulations for this exact loop are not detailed. When cycling in the Médoc Regional Natural Park, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near private property, and to ensure they are comfortable with the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Médoc Regional Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required to access the Médoc Regional Natural Park or to cycle on its public paths, including this loop. Regional Natural Parks in France are designed for public enjoyment and conservation, with free access for activities like cycling.

faqs.question: Are there cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options near the Salaunes et Landes de Bordeaux loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, Salaunes and other villages within the Médoc Regional Natural Park offer various amenities. You can expect to find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the towns and villages along or near the cycling routes. It's advisable to check specific opening hours, especially outside of peak tourist season.
